title: Numerical Error, Roots, Interpolation, and Quadrature · Unfamiliar Decision Lab
summary: Build, test, and communicate a model involving precision-sensitive scientific computation. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a reproducible numerical foundations computational study with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Formulate conditioning stability in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive root finding in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute interpolation splines in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-advanced cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Produce a checked model result
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: For a 2×2 matrix, determinant = ad − bc. Use (3 × 6) − (1 × 2).

content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.
